rows.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-I
rows.note: Leftmost circuit is the standard TTL sink-driver configuration; TTL sinks LED current more reliably than it sources it.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xxv
rows.note: None of the listed statements is generally true for Newton-Raphson convergence.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 11a
rows.note: Lagrange interpolation at x=301 gives approximately 2.4785971428571423.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 11b
rows.note: Composite Simpson rule with h=0.25 gives approximately 0.6932539683, so the 3-decimal answer is 0.693.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1991 | Question: 02,i
rows.note: Match: IEEE 488 -> R, IEEE 796 -> S, IEEE 696 -> Q, RS232-C -> P.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1991 | Question: 04,i,ii,iii,iv
rows.note: Short answers: (i) y := x > 5; (ii) S; while not B do S; (iii) optimal BST root stop, left if, right while; (iv) every subsystem remains consistent, but not necessarily complete.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1993 | Question: 01.4
rows.note: FORTRAN 77 spacing and implicit typing make (a), (c), and (d) valid, while (b) is invalid.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1993 | Question: 02.2
rows.note: Using the root test on ((3m)!/(m!)^3) * x^(3m) gives radius 1/3.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1993 | Question: 02.4
rows.note: A single second-order Runge-Kutta step with h=0.1 gives y(0.1)=0.005.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1993 | Question: 2.5
rows.note: FORTRAN 77 gives implicit integer type for I; with integer division 3/4=0, the assigned value is -2.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1993 | Question: 02.8
rows.note: By the divergence theorem, div(V)=1 over the unit cube, so the flux is 1.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1993 | Question: 02.10
rows.note: Laplace transform: -1 / ((s^2 + 1)(e^(pi s) - 1)).

rows.title: GATE CSE 1993 | Question: 7.1,2,3
rows.note: Subanswers: 7.1 -> C, 7.2 -> B, 7.3 -> A.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1993 | Question: 7.5
rows.note: The fragment causes a runtime error due to nil-pointer dereferencing.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1993 | Question: 20
rows.note: Function: read 5 bytes from input port 20H and store them sequentially starting at the address loaded into HL from memory locations 5000H/5001H. Registers: B is the loop counter, A holds the input byte, HL is the destination pointer.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1994 | Question: 1.3
rows.note: Backward Euler uses y_(n+1) = y_n + h f(x_(n+1), y_(n+1)).

rows.title: GATE CSE 1994 | Question: 3.1
rows.note: False. Address/data multiplexing reduces pin count, not instruction execution time.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1994 | Question: 3.10
rows.note: Match: ECL -> (c), GaAs -> (a), TTL -> (d), CMOS -> (b).

rows.title: GATE CSE 1995 | Question: 1.13
rows.note: Only <> is definitely a complete token without inspecting the next character.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1995 | Question: 2.11
rows.note: Each record uses 4 bytes for the fixed integer plus max(8,16)=16 bytes for the variant part, so 100 records need 2000 bytes.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1995 | Question: 10
rows.note: Part (a): inside 'with A do', names A and B resolve to fields of record A; inside 'with B do', names A and B resolve to fields of record B. Part (b): '2.5A' is the lexical error; the remaining issues are not lexical.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1995 | Question: 16
rows.note: Function: search up to 10 bytes starting at 1C40H for value 05H. Initial registers: A=05H, B=0AH. Final HL points to the first matching byte, or to 1C4AH if no match is found.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1996 | Question: 6
rows.note: The loop continuously outputs an incrementing count to port 00H; the lowest three bits cycle as 000, 001, 010, 011, 100, 101, 110, 111, then repeat.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 4.3
rows.note: Forward Euler on y'=v and v'=f(t) gives y0=0, y1=0, y2=h^2 f(0), y3=2 h^2 f(0) + h^2 f(h); option B is the intended scan despite the dropped h^2 before f(h).

rows.title: GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 4.8
rows.note: In S1 the accessible variables are x of B, z, and y; in S2 they are x of A, i, and y.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 7
rows.note: Legacy design prompt preserved as subjective: derive the FFH output-port interface using D3 and generate a square wave with 7-bus-cycle on/off timing.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 8
rows.note: Legacy Pascal fill-in-the-boxes prompt preserved as subjective.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 22
rows.note: One unambiguous grammar is: S -> id := E | while E do S | begin L end; L -> S | S ; L; E -> A | A < A; A -> A + F | F; F -> id.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 24
rows.note: Legacy Pascal scope/call-visibility prompt preserved as subjective; the scanned question does not expose a single machine-readable key in the current dataset.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1999 | Question: 16
rows.note: Legacy runtime-stack snapshot prompt preserved as subjective.

rows.title: GATE CSE 1999 | Question: 18
rows.note: Legacy 8085 memory-design prompt preserved as subjective: implement 2K x 8 from four 1K x 4 chips over 1000H-17FFH using two chip pairs and address decoding.

rows.title: GATE CSE 2002 | Question: 2.4
rows.note: 5DH + 6BH = C8H, so AC=1 and CY=0.

rows.title: GATE CSE 2003 | Question: 42
rows.note: From the plotted tangents: x0 converges to 1.3, x1 to 0.6, and x2 to 1.3.

rows.title: GATE CSE 2003 | Question: 71
rows.note: The program corresponds to forall x (((exists y)(B(x,y) and C(y))) => A(x)) and not exists x B(x,x).

rows.title: GATE CSE 2007 | Question: 28
rows.note: This is Newton-Raphson for x^2 - 9/4 = 0, so the sequence converges to the positive root 1.5.
